Who I work with

Attorneys and LNC firms,
both plaintiff and defense

Legal nurse consulting has been a recognized discipline since the 1980s. Jeanie brings modern clinical breadth to a well-established field — available to attorneys directly or as a capacity partner for existing LNC practices.

Attorneys & law firms

Whether you're a solo plaintiff attorney or a large defense firm, Jeanie provides direct clinical support on a per-case or ongoing basis — no middleman, clear deliverables, fast turnaround.

  • Personal injury & medical malpractice
  • Workers' compensation
  • Product liability & toxic torts
  • Elder abuse & long-term care cases
  • Wrongful death
  • Per-case billing or retainer arrangements

LNC firms & solo consultants

Jeanie is available as a trusted subcontract partner for established LNC practices that need to expand capacity without adding overhead. White-label ready, NDA-friendly, consistent output.

  • Overflow and surge case capacity
  • White-label under your firm's brand
  • NDA-compliant, confidential workflow
  • Perioperative, women's health & telemetry expertise
  • Deliverables formatted to your specifications
  • Reliable, deadline-driven output
How it works

From first contact to delivered work product

Simple, fast, zero friction. Most cases are underway within 48 hours of the first call.

1

Free consultation

15-minute call — describe your case, Jeanie recommends the right expert role and scopes the engagement. No commitment.

2

Agreement & records

Signed engagement agreement. You send the medical records securely. Jeanie confirms scope, timeline, and fixed price if applicable.

3

Clinical analysis

Jeanie reviews records with active clinical eyes — flagging gaps, standard-of-care issues, and strategic opportunities your legal team can use.

4

Written deliverable

Attorney-ready report delivered on time. Attorney-annotated + clean expert versions. Detailed invoice. Available for follow-up questions.

Case types

Litigation areas Jeanie supports

LNCs are an established part of the litigation team across a wide range of medically-involved case types.

Personal injury

Motor vehicle accidents, slip-and-fall, traumatic injury — medical record review, causation analysis, and damages support.

Medical malpractice

Standard of care analysis, nursing negligence opinions, and breach of duty evaluation for plaintiff and defense.

Workers' compensation

Injury causation, treatment appropriateness, functional capacity, and IME coordination for occupational injury cases.

Product liability

Medical device failures, pharmaceutical litigation, adverse event analysis, and drug recall cases.

Wrongful death

Chronological reconstruction of events, cause of death analysis, and standard of care review in fatal outcome cases.

Elder abuse & long-term care

Pressure ulcer cases, fall prevention, medication errors, and neglect in skilled nursing and long-term care facilities.

Toxic torts

Environmental exposure, chemical injury, occupational illness — linking clinical presentation to exposure events.

Hospital & facility liability

Institutional negligence, staffing issues, policy violations, and patient safety failures within healthcare facilities.

Transparent pricing

Rates

All rates are hourly, in USD, billed per engagement role. Every engagement includes a written deliverable and a detailed invoice. Free initial consultation — always.

Role 1 · Consulting Expert
$150
per hour · 2-hour minimum
  • Entirely behind the scenes
  • Report is an educational tool — not evidence
  • Hired for general nursing knowledge
  • Medical record review & analysis
  • Case screening & merit reports
  • Chronologies, narratives, SOC analysis
Role 2 · Subject Matter Expert
$200
per hour · 2-hour minimum
  • Entirely behind the scenes
  • Report is an educational tool — not evidence
  • Hired for specific clinical specialty
  • Perioperative, cardiac, women's health, telehealth
  • IME support & life care plan analysis
  • No minimum years in specialty required
Role 3 · Testifying Expert
$350
per hour · deposition & trial
  • Retained to testify as expert witness
  • Opinions & reports disclosed as evidence
  • Hired for specific clinical specialty
  • Deposition questions & trial exhibits
  • Opinions used directly in case strategy
  • No minimum years in specialty required

Project-based & retainer pricing also available

For defined-scope projects (case merit packages, full record reviews, life care plan support), Jeanie offers fixed project pricing — no surprise billing. Law firms and LNC practices with consistent volume can arrange monthly retainer agreements, typically $2,000–$5,000/month depending on scope and role mix. Contact Jeanie to discuss.

Not sure which role your case requires? See the full role breakdown or contact Jeanie — she'll recommend the right engagement for your case and budget.

How legal nurses work

Three expert roles — one practice

Legal nurse consulting has three distinct expert roles, each with a different level of visibility, a different purpose, and a different rate. Every role values the nurse as an educator — the attorney relies on clinical knowledge to understand what happened medically and why it matters. Jeanie is available across all three roles depending on the case's needs.

01
Consulting Expert
$150/hr
Hourly rate
Most common LNC role

The most widely used role in legal nurse consulting. The consulting expert works entirely behind the scenes — educating the attorney on the clinical picture, identifying strengths and weaknesses in the case, and producing written analysis used as an internal study tool. Reports and opinions are never disclosed as evidence.

  • Completely behind the scenes
  • Never testifies in any proceeding
  • Report is NOT disclosed as evidence
  • Report used as an educational tool only
  • Hired for general nursing knowledge
  • Every nursing background qualifies
Many legal nurses choose to work exclusively in this role throughout their entire career — it is the backbone of the LNC industry and the ideal starting point for new consultants.
02
Subject Matter Expert
$200/hr
Hourly rate
Specialty-specific role

A strong fit for nurses with specialized clinical backgrounds. The subject matter expert is hired specifically because of their expertise in a particular field — not for general nursing knowledge. Like the consulting expert, this role is entirely behind the scenes; reports and opinions remain internal educational tools, never disclosed as evidence.

  • Completely behind the scenes
  • Never testifies in any proceeding
  • Report is NOT disclosed as evidence
  • Report used as an educational tool only
  • Hired for specific nursing background
  • No minimum years required in specialty
Jeanie's backgrounds in perioperative, women's health, telemetry, and telehealth each qualify for subject matter expert engagements in their respective case types — without requiring decades of specialty experience.
03
Testifying Expert
$350/hr
Hourly rate
Highest-stakes role

The testifying expert is retained to form opinions, prepare reports that are disclosed as evidence, and testify as an expert witness in deposition or at trial. The attorney relies directly on the nurse's first-hand clinical knowledge in a specific field to build their case theory and present it to judge and jury.

  • Retained to testify as expert witness
  • Opinions and reports disclosed as evidence
  • Opinions used directly in litigation strategy
  • Hired for specific nursing background
  • No minimum years required in specialty
  • First-hand clinical experience is key
The attorney relies upon the nurse's direct clinical experience in a specific field to form opinions, prepare reports that become evidence, and testify. Specialized backgrounds — perioperative, cardiac, women's health — are particularly valuable here.
Services

What Jeanie delivers

Standard LNC services with detailed written deliverables. Available remotely to attorneys, firms, and LNC practices across California.

Medical record review & analysis

Comprehensive review, indexing, and chronological organization of medical records. Inconsistencies flagged, missing records identified, key clinical facts surfaced in attorney-ready format.

Standard of care analysis

Expert evaluation of whether physicians, nurses, or facilities met the applicable standard of care. Written nursing standard of care opinions for plaintiff and defense in malpractice and negligence cases.

Case screening & merit reports

Early-stage clinical assessment to determine case viability before committing legal resources. Identifies strengths, weaknesses, and potential defendants — saving time and protecting your docket.

Medical chronologies & narratives

Clear, detailed timelines and medical narrative summaries drawn from voluminous patient records — formatted for attorneys, paralegals, and expert witnesses to use immediately.

IME support & coordination

Independent Medical Examination preparation and coordination — including medical record preparation, pre-IME analysis, and post-IME report review on behalf of the legal team.

Industry-standard LNC service since the 1980s

Damages assessment

Detailed analysis of past and future medical costs, care needs, functional limitations, and long-term impact — providing the clinical foundation to support maximum damages recovery.

Life care plan support

Clinical analysis of future medical needs and associated costs for catastrophic injury, chronic illness, and disability cases — supporting life care planners and damages calculations.

For catastrophic & long-term injury cases

Expert witness support

Identification and coordination of testifying experts, deposition question development, deposition summary review, and visual trial exhibits that make complex medical facts understandable to juries.

Medical research & literature review

Evidence-based literature searches and clinical guideline analysis to support case theory, standard of care arguments, and expert witness opinions.

What you actually receive

Every engagement includes a written deliverable

Not a verbal opinion. Not an AI summary. A professional written work product — attorney-ready, clinically defensible, delivered on time.

Role 1 · 2 · 3

Medical Chronology

Complete chronological narrative organized by date, provider, and facility. Key clinical events highlighted. Documentation gaps flagged. Attorney-annotated version with strategic insights included alongside a clean expert copy.

Role 1 · 2 · 3

Standard of Care Opinion

Written nursing SOC analysis with specific citations to clinical guidelines, hospital policies, and nursing practice standards. Breach of duty identified and explained in language your expert witness and jury can follow.

Role 1 · 2

Case Merit Report

Early-stage clinical assessment identifying strengths, weaknesses, red flags, and missing records. Helps you decide whether to take the case and which experts to pursue — before committing resources to litigation.

Role 1 · 2 · 3

Record Review & Index

Fully organized, indexed medical records with a master table of contents. Missing records identified. Duplicates removed. Relevant records flagged — hours of skimming time eliminated for your entire legal team.

Role 2 · 3

Damages Analysis

Past and future medical costs documented. Functional limitations, ongoing care needs, and long-term impact assessed. Supports maximum damages calculation with clinically defensible cost projections your expert can use at trial.

Role 1 · 2 · 3

IME Report Review

Independent clinical review of opposing IME reports — identifying inaccuracies, methodology issues, and unsupported conclusions. Gives your attorney the clinical ammunition to challenge the opposing expert in deposition and at trial.

Sample work product — Medical Chronology excerpt

This is a redacted sample of the format Jeanie delivers on every chronology engagement. Attorney-annotated version shown.

MEDICAL CHRONOLOGY — Personal Injury Case
Prepared by: Jeanie Vatelia, BSN, RN — Nurse Jeanie Legal  ·  Confidential Attorney Work Product  ·  Role 1 — Consulting Expert
Patient: ████████████  ·  DOB: ██/██/████  ·  Incident Date: ██/██/████
Day 1 · 14:32
Patient presents to ED via EMS following MVA. GCS 14 on arrival. Cervical collar applied in field. Vitals: BP 138/88, HR 102, O2 sat 97% on room air. Initial nursing assessment documents complaint of neck pain 8/10, right shoulder pain, and headache.
⚠ ATTORNEY NOTE: HR 102 on arrival — tachycardia may indicate pain severity and/or compensatory response. Flag for damages argument.
Day 1 · 16:15
Radiology report documents C5-C6 disc herniation. Physician note states "patient counseled on diagnosis and treatment options." No documentation of informed consent discussion in nursing notes.
🔴 DOCUMENTATION GAP: Nursing notes do not corroborate informed consent discussion. Standard of care requires nursing documentation of patient education and consent process. This gap is clinically significant.
Day 1 · 22:40
Night shift nursing note: "Patient sleeping comfortably, vitals stable, no acute distress noted." Vital signs documented q4h per protocol.
✓ CLINICAL NOTE: Appropriate monitoring frequency for this acuity. Nursing documentation consistent with standard of care at this interval.
Day 3 · AM
MISSING RECORDS IDENTIFIED: Physical therapy evaluation ordered Day 2 — no PT notes present in records provided. Recommend requesting complete PT documentation from ████████████ facility. This documentation is critical to establishing ongoing functional limitation.
How this deliverable is formatted: Every chronology includes (1) the attorney-annotated version above with clinical flags and strategic notes, (2) a clean version for expert witnesses without attorney notes, and (3) a missing records list with specific recommendations. Delivered as a formatted PDF and editable document.
Common questions

FAQ for attorneys

The questions Jeanie hears most often — answered directly.

Do I need a Legal Nurse Consultant?
If your case involves medical records, diagnoses, treatment decisions, or clinical standards — yes. Attorneys who review medical records without clinical support routinely miss inconsistencies, documentation gaps, and standard-of-care violations that opposing counsel finds. An LNC pays for itself on the first case.
What does a deliverable actually look like?
Every engagement includes a written work product: a chronology, standard-of-care opinion, case screening report, or IME review — depending on what you need. Reports are delivered in attorney-ready format with key findings highlighted, questions flagged, and clinical issues explained in plain language your entire legal team can use immediately.
How long does a case review take?
Standard turnaround is 5–10 business days for a typical medical record review, depending on volume. Rush turnaround (2–3 days) is available for urgent cases. Timeline is confirmed at the start of every engagement — no surprises.
Do you work plaintiff AND defense?
Yes. Jeanie works both sides. The clinical analysis is the same regardless of which side retains her — what happened medically either supports your case theory or it doesn't. Being honest about that upfront is what makes the work valuable.
Can your reports be used in court?
It depends on the role. In Role 1 (Consulting Expert) and Role 2 (Subject Matter Expert), reports are internal educational tools — never disclosed as evidence. In Role 3 (Testifying Expert), Jeanie forms opinions, prepares reports that are disclosed as evidence, and testifies. You choose the role based on your case strategy.
Is everything HIPAA compliant and confidential?
Yes. All engagements are fully HIPAA compliant. Jeanie operates under a strict confidentiality framework and will sign an NDA for subcontract arrangements. No case information is ever shared, discussed, or referenced outside of your direct engagement.
What if my case needs a specialty I don't see listed?
Call and ask — (909) 638-2111. Jeanie's backgrounds span perioperative, women's reproductive health, cardiac telemetry, pain management, acute care, and telehealth. If your case falls outside her direct expertise, she will tell you honestly and can help identify the right specialist through her network.
How is billing handled?
Hourly engagements are billed with a detailed invoice after work is completed. Project-based engagements are scoped and fixed-priced upfront — no surprise billing. Retainer arrangements are invoiced monthly. A signed agreement is in place before any work begins.

Why Jeanie does this work

Medicine took something from her family.
Now she makes sure answers can't be hidden.

Jeanie and her twin sister came into this world together — and the very next morning, their mother was gone. No clear explanation. No real answers. A family left to grieve while the medical record said nothing useful.

That silence became the quiet engine behind everything Jeanie has built. Raised by their biological mother's sister and her husband — two people who stepped in with unconditional love and raised both girls as their own — Jeanie grew up understanding what it truly means when medicine fails someone and no one can explain why.

When the father who raised her became seriously ill before she ever started nursing school, Jeanie didn't feel helpless for long — she enrolled. She wanted to understand what was happening to him. To be able to advocate for her family the way her family had always shown up for her.

Over the next decade she built her clinical career across the most demanding settings in healthcare — cardiac telemetry at Kaiser Permanente and Eisenhower Medical Center, surgical and women's health at Planned Parenthood across multiple facilities, travel nursing through Aya Healthcare in telemetry and medical-surgical units, ambulatory surgery, and telehealth mental health support. She has seen what excellent medicine looks like, and she knows precisely when it falls short.

Legal nursing isn't a career pivot for Jeanie. It's the culmination of a life spent needing the truth about what happens inside a medical record — and making sure families, and the attorneys who fight for them, finally get it.

Personal stake in medical truth
Raised by love, driven by purpose
10+ years across clinical settings

Not an algorithm

Real nurse. Real bedside. This week.

AI legal nurse "consultant" tools are multiplying fast. As of 2025, a fifth of law firms are experimenting with generative AI for medical-legal work — and the results have been disastrous for attorneys who relied on them. Hallucinations. Missed context. Reports that sound right but are clinically wrong.

Jeanie practiced bedside nursing at Eisenhower Health this week. She monitored IV analgesics, titrated medications, and assessed hemodynamic changes in real patients with real conditions. When she tells you what the standard of care was — she's not quoting a dataset trained on 2019 records. She's telling you what she did last Tuesday.

That's not just a differentiator. In deposition, it's the answer that holds up.

AI / Bot toolsTrained on historical datasets. No current clinical context. Known for hallucinations and missed nuance. One attorney reported errors and then couldn't reach the company for months.
Nurse Jeanie LegalActive bedside RN at Eisenhower Health. Every report written by Jeanie personally. Current standards, real-world protocols, defensible opinions.
Junior or inactive LNCsNearly 75% of LNCs have less than a decade of experience. Many left bedside nursing early and are no longer current on evolving care standards.
Jeanie's clinical currency10+ years. Currently practicing. Standards of care knowledge is from this year — not when she last touched a patient years ago.
Generic "LNC firms"Cases routed to whoever is available. No guarantee of specialty match. Work product quality varies by who picks up the case.
Direct with JeanieEvery case personally reviewed and worked by Jeanie. You know exactly who is on your case — and you can call her directly at (909) 638-2111.
